authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Beardsley Dam downstream to Spring Gap footbridge (incl. Beardsley Afterbay)
reaches.toDesc: U.S. Forest Service footbridge at Spring Gap

reaches.name: Spring Gap footbridge downstream to New Melones Reservoir
reaches.fromDesc: U.S. Forest Service footbridge at Spring Gap

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(147) — Stanislaus River, Middle Fork (Tuolumne Co.)
sources.quotedText: (147) Stanislaus River, Middle Fork (Tuolumne Co.). (A) From Beardsley Dam downstream to the U. S. Forest Service footbridge at Spring Gap (including the Beardsley Afterbay). All year. Only artificial lures may be used. 2 trout (B) From the U.S. Forest Service footbridge at Spring Gap to New Melones Reservoir. All year. 2 trout. 4 trout in possession.

sources.title: Middle Fork Stanislaus River Wild Trout Management Plan
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (updated June 1, 2026)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW
sources.quotedText: R4 Tuolumne Stanislaus River Middle Fork Rainbow Trout Catchable Put and Take April-September

sources.title: Middle Fork Stanislaus River — Wikipedia (infobox mouth coordinates 38°09′15″N 120°21′27″W, sourced to GNIS Feature ID 234292); used because the USGS GNIS ArcGIS Streams (Mouth) layer has no exact-name record for this tributary. Corrects prior file coordinates (-120.1200, 38.1500), which were an unsourced estimate roughly 21 km from the river's mouth at its confluence with the Stanislaus River in New Melones Reservoir.

regulations.humanSummary: Beardsley Dam to Spring Gap footbridge (incl. Beardsley Afterbay) open to fishing all year
regulations.verbatimText: From Beardsley Dam downstream to the U. S. Forest Service footbridge at Spring Gap (including the Beardsley Afterbay). All year.

parameters.note: Only artificial lures may be used (barbless hooks not specifically required in this subsection).

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures may be used from Beardsley Dam to Spring Gap footbridge
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 trout daily bag limit; this subsection does not separately state a distinct possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 trout daily bag limit from Beardsley Dam to Spring Gap footbridge
Spring Gap footbridge to New Melones Reservoir open to fishing all year
regulations.verbatimText: From the U.S. Forest Service footbridge at Spring Gap to New Melones Reservoir. All year.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 trout per day, 4 trout in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 trout per day, 4 in possession from Spring Gap footbridge to New Melones Reservoir

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Stanislaus River Middle Fork (Region 4, Tuolumne Co.) for catchable-size Rainbow Trout under the Put and Take program, with a stated release month range of April-September. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
